parameters.name: sourcegrp
parameters.label: Source Group
parameters.description: If there are input groups, specifying a group name in this field will cause this to act only upon the group specified for the source. Accepts patterns, as described in .

parameters.name: xsectiongrp
parameters.label: X-Section Group
parameters.description: If there are input groups, specifying a group name in this field will cause this to act only upon the group specified for the cross-section. Accepts patterns, as described in .

parameters.name: dofuse
parameters.label: Fuse Points
parameters.description: This should almost always be turned on when cross-sections are used. It consolidates points of polygons that would otherwise cross or overlap when the bevel takes place.

parameters.name: fronttype
parameters.label: Front Face
parameters.description: Control how the front face of the extrusion should be built. You may wish to have a "No Output" because some faces are never actually seen when doing animation and, therefore, would only take up addit

parameters.name: backtype
parameters.label: Back Face
parameters.description: This value controls whether or not the back of the extruded object will have a face or not. The options are the same as the Front Face options above.

parameters.name: sidetype
parameters.label: Side Mesh
parameters.description: Controls how the cross-section(s) will be extruded. If the input cross-section is a Bzier or NURBS curve, the surface will be constructed with a patch of the same geometry type.

parameters.name: initextrude
parameters.label: Initialize Extrusion
parameters.description: If the cross-section face that you created doesn't match up nicely to the size of the geometry you are extruding, this command will scale and translate it so that it fits nicely.

parameters.name: thickxlate
parameters.label: Thickness Translate
parameters.description: Shifts the cross-section profile perpendicularly to the normals of the input geometry. This relates to the X axis of the cross-section profile. When used with text, this typically has the effect of ma

parameters.name: thickscale
parameters.label: Thickness Scale
parameters.description: Scales the cross-section profile perpendicularly to the normals of the input geometry. This is equivalent to scaling the cross-section in its X axis. This parameter has no effect on the default profil

parameters.name: depthxlate
parameters.label: Depth Translate
parameters.description: Moves the cross-section in the direction of the normal. Positive values will move backwards to the direction of the normal. Depth movement relates to the Y axis of the input cross-section. Using this

parameters.name: depthscale
parameters.label: Depth Scale
parameters.description: Scales the cross-section in the direction of the source geometry's normals. This is equivalent to scaling the input cross-section in its Y axis and will determine how deep (thick) the resulting extrus

parameters.name: vertex
parameters.label: Vertex
parameters.description: Translates the cross-section such that the vertex specified is at the cross-section origin.

parameters.name: docusp
parameters.label: Cusp Polygonal Sides
parameters.description: Determines whether or not sides are to be smooth-shaded or faceted using the angle value in Side Cusp Angle field.

parameters.name: cuspangle
parameters.label: Side Cusp Angle
parameters.description: When checked, this value will control the angle at which faceting of the sides will occur. A value of 20 is default.

parameters.name: sharefaces
parameters.label: Consolidate Faces to Mesh
parameters.description: If selected the extrusion will share points between the front face and the first row of the side mesh and between the last face and the last row of the side mesh.

parameters.name: removesharedsides
parameters.label: Remove Shared Sides
parameters.description: Prevents the creation of duplicate sides.

parameters.name: newg
parameters.label: Create Output Groups
parameters.description: When this option is checked, it causes the Extrude to generate three new groups representing the primitives belonging to the front faces, back faces, and the side bevel/extrusion. The name of the grou

parameters.name: frontgrp
parameters.label: Front Group
parameters.description: Output group name to create for front face geometry.

parameters.name: backgrp
parameters.label: Back Group
parameters.description: Output group name to create for back face geometry.

parameters.name: sidegrp
parameters.label: Side Group
parameters.description: Output group name to create for side bevel/extrude geometry.

parameters.name: normals
parameters.label: Normals
parameters.description: Help Not Available.
